{{Infobox noble | name = Ikatsu no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Nakatomi no Ikatsu, Ikatsu Omimi, Rai Daijin no Mikoto | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | Rank = | lord = [[Emperor Chūai]], [[Empress Jingū]], [[Emperor Ingyō]]? | clan = [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]] (Kawachi Province), {{ill|Iki clan|ja|壱岐氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki clan|qq=}}, Wu nobility in Yamashiro Province, Shinetsu clan in Settsu Province, {{ill|Ikuta clan|ja|生田氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikuta clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Settsu Province), {{ill|Nakatomi Kurihara clan|ja|中臣栗原氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi Kurihara clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tsushima clan|ja|津島直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Settsu Province), Mimami clan (Kawachi Province)<ref name="#1">*[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]</ref> | parents = Father: {{ill|Omi Sayama no Mikoto|ja|臣狭山命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Sayama no Mikoto|qq=}}<ref name="#2">*[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]</ref><ref name="#3">Matsuo Shrine Genealogy (in *{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従}}*, vol. 181)</ref> | siblings = | spouse = Woman of Baekje (or Silla) | children = {{ill|Ōkogashi no Mikoto|ja|大小橋命|1=Ōbashi no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkogashi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbashi no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Maneko no Mikoto|ja|真根子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maneko no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Dainishi no Mikoto|ja|弟子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainishi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="#1"/>, {{ill|Nihon Daijin no Mikoto|ja|日本大臣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Daijin no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="#2"/><ref name="#3"/> }}
'''Ikatsu no Mikoto''' (中臣 烏賊津) is a legendary noble and regarded as the founder of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣連|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣氏}} from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Although not mentioned in the *[[:en:Kojiki|Records of Ancient Matters]]*, he is referred to in the *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]]* under various names such as '''Nakatomi no Ikatsu Omimi''' and '''Rai Daijin no Mikoto'''. Genealogical texts such as *[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]* identify him as the son of Omi Sayama no Mikoto and father of {{ill|Ōkogashi no Mikoto|ja|大小橋命|1=Ōbashi no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkogashi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbashi no Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Maneko no Mikoto|ja|真根子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maneko no Mikoto|qq=}}.
== Records ==
According to the *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]]* (vol. 8), upon the sudden death of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] kept his death secret with the help of senior officials, including {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikatsu|ja|中臣烏賊津|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Ikatsu|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōmiwa no Ōtomonushi|ja|大三輪大友主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiwa no Ōtomonushi|qq=}}, and others. Ikatsu no Mikoto was tasked with protecting the court during this period.<ref>*[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]], Emperor Chūai, year 9, February 5 entry.</ref>
Later, in vol. 9 of the *Chronicles*, Empress Jingū chose Ikatsu no Mikoto to perform sacred divinations (審神者) during her preparation for an important ritual.<ref>*[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]], Empress Jingū, pre-regency record (Emperor Chūai, year 9, March 1 entry).</ref> His role as a mediator between gods and humans is emphasized, reflecting the meaning of the term "Nakatomi."
Subsequent records in the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]] mention a courtier named Nakatomi no Ikatsu Omimi, who is believed by some scholars to either be a different person with the same name or the same individual as Ikatsu no Mikoto.<ref>*[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]</ref>
== Tsushima Legends ==
Several legends about Ikatsu no Mikoto are preserved on [[:en:Tsushima Island|Tsushima Island]]:<ref>Tsuzuki Tōzō, *Shinto in Tsushima* (San-Ichi Shobō, 1972).</ref> * {{ill|Raijin Shrine|ja|雷神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin Shrine|qq=}} in Izuhara Town commemorates Ikatsu no Mikoto as the figure who managed foreign tribute after returning from Silla. * {{ill|Raimyo Shrine|ja|雷命神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raimyo Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Izuhara is linked to his descendants. * {{ill|Tai Norito Shrine|ja|太祝詞神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tai Norito Shrine|qq=}} in Mitsushima Town is believed to house his grave. * {{ill|Hekireki Shrine|ja|霹靂神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hekireki Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} marks the place of his arrival from Silla. * {{ill|Norito Shrine|ja|能理刀神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Norito Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is where he is said to have performed divinations.
== Genealogy ==
Ikatsu no Mikoto is identified as the son of {{ill|Omi Sayama no Mikoto|ja|臣狭山命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Sayama no Mikoto|qq=}}. His children include {{ill|Ōkogashi no Mikoto|ja|大小橋命|1=Ōbashi no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkogashi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbashi no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Maneko no Mikoto|ja|真根子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maneko no Mikoto|qq=}}, and {{ill|Nihon Daijin no Mikoto|ja|日本大臣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Daijin no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Some accounts mention his marriage to a woman from Baekje or Silla, with their descendants linked to several prominent clans.<ref>*[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * *Chronicles of Japan*, Vol. 1, Iwanami Bunko, 1994. * Ujitani Takeshi (trans.), *Chronicles of Japan*, Kodansha Academic, 1988. * Saeki Arikiyo, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans*, Yuzankaku, 2015. * Kishi Toshio (ed.), *Ancient Japan: Government and Ritual*, Chūō Kōron Bunko, 1996.
== See also == * [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]]

'''Ikatsu no Mikoto''' (中臣 烏賊津) is a legendary noble and regarded as the founder of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣連|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣氏}} from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Although not mentioned in the *[[:en:Kojiki|Records of Ancient Matters]]*, he is referred to in the *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]]* under various names such as '''Nakatomi no Ikatsu Omimi''' and '''Rai Daijin no Mikoto'''. Genealogical texts such as *[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]* identify him as the son of Omi Sayama no Mikoto and father of {{ill|Ōkogashi no Mikoto|ja|大小橋命|1=Ōbashi no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkogashi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbashi no Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Maneko no Mikoto|ja|真根子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maneko no Mikoto|qq=}}.
== Records ==
According to the *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]]* (vol. 8), upon the sudden death of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] kept his death secret with the help of senior officials, including {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikatsu|ja|中臣烏賊津|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Ikatsu|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōmiwa no Ōtomonushi|ja|大三輪大友主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiwa no Ōtomonushi|qq=}}, and others. Ikatsu no Mikoto was tasked with protecting the court during this period.<ref>*[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]], Emperor Chūai, year 9, February 5 entry.</ref>
Later, in vol. 9 of the *Chronicles*, Empress Jingū chose Ikatsu no Mikoto to perform sacred divinations (審神者) during her preparation for an important ritual.<ref>*[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]], Empress Jingū, pre-regency record (Emperor Chūai, year 9, March 1 entry).</ref> His role as a mediator between gods and humans is emphasized, reflecting the meaning of the term "Nakatomi."
Subsequent records in the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]] mention a courtier named Nakatomi no Ikatsu Omimi, who is believed by some scholars to either be a different person with the same name or the same individual as Ikatsu no Mikoto.<ref>*[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]</ref>
== Tsushima Legends ==
Several legends about Ikatsu no Mikoto are preserved on [[:en:Tsushima Island|Tsushima Island]]:<ref>Tsuzuki Tōzō, *Shinto in Tsushima* (San-Ichi Shobō, 1972).</ref> * {{ill|Raijin Shrine|ja|雷神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin Shrine|qq=}} in Izuhara Town commemorates Ikatsu no Mikoto as the figure who managed foreign tribute after returning from Silla. * {{ill|Raimyo Shrine|ja|雷命神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raimyo Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Izuhara is linked to his descendants. * {{ill|Tai Norito Shrine|ja|太祝詞神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tai Norito Shrine|qq=}} in Mitsushima Town is believed to house his grave. * {{ill|Hekireki Shrine|ja|霹靂神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hekireki Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} marks the place of his arrival from Silla. * {{ill|Norito Shrine|ja|能理刀神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Norito Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is where he is said to have performed divinations.
== Genealogy ==
Ikatsu no Mikoto is identified as the son of {{ill|Omi Sayama no Mikoto|ja|臣狭山命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Sayama no Mikoto|qq=}}. His children include {{ill|Ōkogashi no Mikoto|ja|大小橋命|1=Ōbashi no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkogashi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbashi no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Maneko no Mikoto|ja|真根子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maneko no Mikoto|qq=}}, and {{ill|Nihon Daijin no Mikoto|ja|日本大臣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Daijin no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Some accounts mention his marriage to a woman from Baekje or Silla, with their descendants linked to several prominent clans.<ref>*[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * *Chronicles of Japan*, Vol. 1, Iwanami Bunko, 1994. * Ujitani Takeshi (trans.), *Chronicles of Japan*, Kodansha Academic, 1988. * Saeki Arikiyo, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans*, Yuzankaku, 2015. * Kishi Toshio (ed.), *Ancient Japan: Government and Ritual*, Chūō Kōron Bunko, 1996.
== See also == * [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q55536814]] Wikidata name: Nakatomi no Ikatsu [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Ikatsu no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Nakatomi no Ikatsu, Ikatsu Omimi, Rai Daijin no Mikoto | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | Rank = | lord = [[Emperor Chūai]], [[Empress Jingū]], [[Emperor Ingyō]]? | clan = [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]] (Kawachi Province), {{ill|Iki clan|ja|壱岐氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki clan|qq=}}, Wu nobility in Yamashiro Province, Shinetsu clan in Settsu Province, {{ill|Ikuta clan|ja|生田氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikuta clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Settsu Province), {{ill|Nakatomi Kurihara clan|ja|中臣栗原氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi Kurihara clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tsushima clan|ja|津島直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Settsu Province), Mimami clan (Kawachi Province)<ref name="#1">*[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]</ref> | parents = Father: {{ill|Omi Sayama no Mikoto|ja|臣狭山命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Sayama no Mikoto|qq=}}<ref name="#2">*[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]</ref><ref name="#3">Matsuo Shrine Genealogy (in *{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従}}*, vol. 181)</ref> | siblings = | spouse = Woman of Baekje (or Silla) | children = {{ill|Ōkogashi no Mikoto|ja|大小橋命|1=Ōbashi no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkogashi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbashi no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Maneko no Mikoto|ja|真根子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maneko no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Dainishi no Mikoto|ja|弟子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainishi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="#1"/>, {{ill|Nihon Daijin no Mikoto|ja|日本大臣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Daijin no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="#2"/><ref name="#3"/> }}
'''Ikatsu no Mikoto''' (中臣 烏賊津) is a legendary noble and regarded as the founder of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣連|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣氏}} from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Although not mentioned in the *[[:en:Kojiki|Records of Ancient Matters]]*, he is referred to in the *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]]* under various names such as '''Nakatomi no Ikatsu Omimi''' and '''Rai Daijin no Mikoto'''. Genealogical texts such as *[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]* identify him as the son of Omi Sayama no Mikoto and father of {{ill|Ōkogashi no Mikoto|ja|大小橋命|1=Ōbashi no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkogashi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbashi no Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Maneko no Mikoto|ja|真根子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maneko no Mikoto|qq=}}.
== Records ==
According to the *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]]* (vol. 8), upon the sudden death of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] kept his death secret with the help of senior officials, including {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikatsu|ja|中臣烏賊津|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Ikatsu|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōmiwa no Ōtomonushi|ja|大三輪大友主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiwa no Ōtomonushi|qq=}}, and others. Ikatsu no Mikoto was tasked with protecting the court during this period.<ref>*[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]], Emperor Chūai, year 9, February 5 entry.</ref>
Later, in vol. 9 of the *Chronicles*, Empress Jingū chose Ikatsu no Mikoto to perform sacred divinations (審神者) during her preparation for an important ritual.<ref>*[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]], Empress Jingū, pre-regency record (Emperor Chūai, year 9, March 1 entry).</ref> His role as a mediator between gods and humans is emphasized, reflecting the meaning of the term "Nakatomi."
Subsequent records in the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]] mention a courtier named Nakatomi no Ikatsu Omimi, who is believed by some scholars to either be a different person with the same name or the same individual as Ikatsu no Mikoto.<ref>*[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]</ref>
== Tsushima Legends ==
Several legends about Ikatsu no Mikoto are preserved on [[:en:Tsushima Island|Tsushima Island]]:<ref>Tsuzuki Tōzō, *Shinto in Tsushima* (San-Ichi Shobō, 1972).</ref> * {{ill|Raijin Shrine|ja|雷神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin Shrine|qq=}} in Izuhara Town commemorates Ikatsu no Mikoto as the figure who managed foreign tribute after returning from Silla. * {{ill|Raimyo Shrine|ja|雷命神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raimyo Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Izuhara is linked to his descendants. * {{ill|Tai Norito Shrine|ja|太祝詞神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tai Norito Shrine|qq=}} in Mitsushima Town is believed to house his grave. * {{ill|Hekireki Shrine|ja|霹靂神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hekireki Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} marks the place of his arrival from Silla. * {{ill|Norito Shrine|ja|能理刀神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Norito Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is where he is said to have performed divinations.
== Genealogy ==
Ikatsu no Mikoto is identified as the son of {{ill|Omi Sayama no Mikoto|ja|臣狭山命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Sayama no Mikoto|qq=}}. His children include {{ill|Ōkogashi no Mikoto|ja|大小橋命|1=Ōbashi no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkogashi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbashi no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Maneko no Mikoto|ja|真根子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maneko no Mikoto|qq=}}, and {{ill|Nihon Daijin no Mikoto|ja|日本大臣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Daijin no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Some accounts mention his marriage to a woman from Baekje or Silla, with their descendants linked to several prominent clans.<ref>*[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * *Chronicles of Japan*, Vol. 1, Iwanami Bunko, 1994. * Ujitani Takeshi (trans.), *Chronicles of Japan*, Kodansha Academic, 1988. * Saeki Arikiyo, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans*, Yuzankaku, 2015. * Kishi Toshio (ed.), *Ancient Japan: Government and Ritual*, Chūō Kōron Bunko, 1996.
== See also == * [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]]

Several legends about Ikatsu no Mikoto are preserved on [[:en:Tsushima Island|Tsushima Island]]:<ref>Tsuzuki Tōzō, *Shinto in Tsushima* (San-Ichi Shobō, 1972).</ref> * {{ill|Raijin Shrine|ja|雷神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin Shrine|qq=}} in Izuhara Town commemorates Ikatsu no Mikoto as the figure who managed foreign tribute after returning from Silla. * {{ill|Raimyo Shrine|ja|雷命神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raimyo Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Izuhara is linked to his descendants. * {{ill|Tai Norito Shrine|ja|太祝詞神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tai Norito Shrine|qq=}} in Mitsushima Town is believed to house his grave. * {{ill|Hekireki Shrine|ja|霹靂神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hekireki Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} marks the place of his arrival from Silla. * {{ill|Norito Shrine|ja|能理刀神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Norito Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is where he is said to have performed divinations.
== Genealogy ==
Ikatsu no Mikoto is identified as the son of {{ill|Omi Sayama no Mikoto|ja|臣狭山命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Sayama no Mikoto|qq=|1=Omisayama no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99517230}}. His children include {{ill|Ōkogashi no Mikoto|ja|大小橋命|1=Ōbashi no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkogashi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbashi no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Maneko no Mikoto|ja|真根子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maneko no Mikoto|qq=|1=Iki no Makoneko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22125699}}, and {{ill|Nihon Daijin no Mikoto|ja|日本大臣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Daijin no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Some accounts mention his marriage to a woman from Baekje or Silla, with their descendants linked to several prominent clans.<ref>*[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]</ref>

{{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|spouse={{ill|Q108616853|qid=Q108616853}}|children={{ill|Q22125699|qid=Q22125699}}; {{ill|Ōbase no Mikoto|qid=Q99517235}}|father={{ill|Omisayama no Mikoto|qid=Q99517230}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q55536814]] Wikidata name: Nakatomi no Ikatsu [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Ikatsu no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Nakatomi no Ikatsu, Ikatsu Omimi, Rai Daijin no Mikoto | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | Rank = | lord = [[Emperor Chūai]], [[Empress Jingū]], [[Emperor Ingyō]]? | clan = [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]] (Kawachi Province), {{ill|Iki clan|ja|壱岐氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki clan|qq=|1=Iki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429628}}, Wu nobility in Yamashiro Province, Shinetsu clan in Settsu Province, {{ill|Ikuta clan|ja|生田氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikuta clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Settsu Province), {{ill|Nakatomi Kurihara clan|ja|中臣栗原氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi Kurihara clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tsushima clan|ja|津島直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Settsu Province), Mimami clan (Kawachi Province)<ref name="#1">*[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]</ref> | parents = Father: {{ill|Omi Sayama no Mikoto|ja|臣狭山命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Sayama no Mikoto|qq=|1=Omisayama no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99517230}}<ref name="#2">*[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]</ref><ref name="#3">Matsuo Shrine Genealogy (in *{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1554852}}*, vol. 181)</ref> | siblings = | spouse = Woman of Baekje (or Silla) | children = {{ill|Ōkogashi no Mikoto|ja|大小橋命|1=Ōbashi no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkogashi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbashi no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Maneko no Mikoto|ja|真根子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maneko no Mikoto|qq=|1=Iki no Makoneko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22125699}}, {{ill|Dainishi no Mikoto|ja|弟子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainishi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="#1"/>, {{ill|Nihon Daijin no Mikoto|ja|日本大臣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Daijin no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="#2"/><ref name="#3"/> }}
'''Ikatsu no Mikoto''' (中臣 烏賊津) is a legendary noble and regarded as the founder of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣連|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣氏}} from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Although not mentioned in the *[[:en:Kojiki|Records of Ancient Matters]]*, he is referred to in the *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]]* under various names such as '''Nakatomi no Ikatsu Omimi''' and '''Rai Daijin no Mikoto'''. Genealogical texts such as *[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]* identify him as the son of Omi Sayama no Mikoto and father of {{ill|Ōkogashi no Mikoto|ja|大小橋命|1=Ōbashi no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkogashi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbashi no Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Maneko no Mikoto|ja|真根子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maneko no Mikoto|qq=|1=Iki no Makoneko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22125699}}.
== Records ==
According to the *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]]* (vol. 8), upon the sudden death of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] kept his death secret with the help of senior officials, including {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikatsu|ja|中臣烏賊津|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Ikatsu|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōmiwa no Ōtomonushi|ja|大三輪大友主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiwa no Ōtomonushi|qq=|1=Otomoneshi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55536810}}, and others. Ikatsu no Mikoto was tasked with protecting the court during this period.<ref>*[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]], Emperor Chūai, year 9, February 5 entry.</ref>
Later, in vol. 9 of the *Chronicles*, Empress Jingū chose Ikatsu no Mikoto to perform sacred divinations (審神者) during her preparation for an important ritual.<ref>*[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]], Empress Jingū, pre-regency record (Emperor Chūai, year 9, March 1 entry).</ref> His role as a mediator between gods and humans is emphasized, reflecting the meaning of the term "Nakatomi."
Subsequent records in the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]] mention a courtier named Nakatomi no Ikatsu Omimi, who is believed by some scholars to either be a different person with the same name or the same individual as Ikatsu no Mikoto.<ref>*[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]</ref>
== Tsushima Legends ==
Several legends about Ikatsu no Mikoto are preserved on [[:en:Tsushima Island|Tsushima Island]]:<ref>Tsuzuki Tōzō, *Shinto in Tsushima* (San-Ichi Shobō, 1972).</ref> * {{ill|Raijin Shrine|ja|雷神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin Shrine|qq=}} in Izuhara Town commemorates Ikatsu no Mikoto as the figure who managed foreign tribute after returning from Silla. * {{ill|Raimyo Shrine|ja|雷命神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raimyo Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Izuhara is linked to his descendants. * {{ill|Tai Norito Shrine|ja|太祝詞神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tai Norito Shrine|qq=}} in Mitsushima Town is believed to house his grave. * {{ill|Hekireki Shrine|ja|霹靂神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hekireki Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} marks the place of his arrival from Silla. * {{ill|Norito Shrine|ja|能理刀神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Norito Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is where he is said to have performed divinations.
== Genealogy ==
Ikatsu no Mikoto is identified as the son of {{ill|Omi Sayama no Mikoto|ja|臣狭山命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Sayama no Mikoto|qq=|1=Omisayama no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99517230}}. His children include {{ill|Ōkogashi no Mikoto|ja|大小橋命|1=Ōbashi no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkogashi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbashi no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Maneko no Mikoto|ja|真根子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maneko no Mikoto|qq=|1=Iki no Makoneko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22125699}}, and {{ill|Nihon Daijin no Mikoto|ja|日本大臣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Daijin no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Some accounts mention his marriage to a woman from Baekje or Silla, with their descendants linked to several prominent clans.<ref>*[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]</ref>

{{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|spouse={{ill|Q108616853|qid=Q108616853}}|children={{ill|Q22125699|qid=Q22125699}}; {{ill|Ōbase no Mikoto|qid=Q99517235}}|father={{ill|Omisayama no Mikoto|qid=Q99517230}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q55536814]] Wikidata name: Nakatomi no Ikatsu [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Ikatsu no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Nakatomi no Ikatsu, Ikatsu Omimi, Rai Daijin no Mikoto | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | Rank = | lord = [[Emperor Chūai]], [[Empress Jingū]], [[Emperor Ingyō]]? | clan = [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]] (Kawachi Province), {{ill|Iki clan|ja|壱岐氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki clan|qq=|1=Iki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429628}}, Wu nobility in Yamashiro Province, Shinetsu clan in Settsu Province, {{ill|Ikuta clan|ja|生田氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikuta clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Settsu Province), {{ill|Nakatomi Kurihara clan|ja|中臣栗原氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi Kurihara clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tsushima clan|ja|津島直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Settsu Province), Mimami clan (Kawachi Province)<ref name="#1">*[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]</ref> | parents = Father: {{ill|Omi Sayama no Mikoto|ja|臣狭山命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Sayama no Mikoto|qq=|1=Omisayama no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99517230}}<ref name="#2">*[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]</ref><ref name="#3">Matsuo Shrine Genealogy (in *{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1554852}}*, vol. 181)</ref> | siblings = | spouse = Woman of Baekje (or Silla) | children = {{ill|Ōkogashi no Mikoto|ja|大小橋命|1=Ōbashi no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkogashi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbashi no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Maneko no Mikoto|ja|真根子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maneko no Mikoto|qq=|1=Iki no Makoneko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22125699}}, {{ill|Dainishi no Mikoto|ja|弟子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainishi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="#1"/>, {{ill|Nihon Daijin no Mikoto|ja|日本大臣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Daijin no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="#2"/><ref name="#3"/> }}
'''Ikatsu no Mikoto''' (中臣 烏賊津) is a legendary noble and regarded as the founder of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣連|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣氏}} from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Although not mentioned in the *[[:en:Kojiki|Records of Ancient Matters]]*, he is referred to in the *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]]* under various names such as '''Nakatomi no Ikatsu Omimi''' and '''Rai Daijin no Mikoto'''. Genealogical texts such as *[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]* identify him as the son of Omi Sayama no Mikoto and father of {{ill|Ōkogashi no Mikoto|ja|大小橋命|1=Ōbashi no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkogashi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbashi no Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Maneko no Mikoto|ja|真根子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maneko no Mikoto|qq=|1=Iki no Makoneko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22125699}}.
== Records ==
According to the *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]]* (vol. 8), upon the sudden death of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] kept his death secret with the help of senior officials, including {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikatsu|ja|中臣烏賊津|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Ikatsu|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōmiwa no Ōtomonushi|ja|大三輪大友主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiwa no Ōtomonushi|qq=|1=Otomoneshi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55536810}}, and others. Ikatsu no Mikoto was tasked with protecting the court during this period.<ref>*[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]], Emperor Chūai, year 9, February 5 entry.</ref>
Later, in vol. 9 of the *Chronicles*, Empress Jingū chose Ikatsu no Mikoto to perform sacred divinations (審神者) during her preparation for an important ritual.<ref>*[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]], Empress Jingū, pre-regency record (Emperor Chūai, year 9, March 1 entry).</ref> His role as a mediator between gods and humans is emphasized, reflecting the meaning of the term "Nakatomi."
Subsequent records in the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]] mention a courtier named Nakatomi no Ikatsu Omimi, who is believed by some scholars to either be a different person with the same name or the same individual as Ikatsu no Mikoto.<ref>*[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]</ref>
== Tsushima Legends ==
Several legends about Ikatsu no Mikoto are preserved on [[:en:Tsushima Island|Tsushima Island]]:<ref>Tsuzuki Tōzō, *Shinto in Tsushima* (San-Ichi Shobō, 1972).</ref> * {{ill|Raijin Shrine|ja|雷神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin Shrine|qq=}} in Izuhara Town commemorates Ikatsu no Mikoto as the figure who managed foreign tribute after returning from Silla. * {{ill|Raimyo Shrine|ja|雷命神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raimyo Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Izuhara is linked to his descendants. * {{ill|Tai Norito Shrine|ja|太祝詞神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tai Norito Shrine|qq=}} in Mitsushima Town is believed to house his grave. * {{ill|Hekireki Shrine|ja|霹靂神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hekireki Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} marks the place of his arrival from Silla. * {{ill|Norito Shrine|ja|能理刀神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Norito Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is where he is said to have performed divinations.
== Genealogy ==
Ikatsu no Mikoto is identified as the son of {{ill|Omi Sayama no Mikoto|ja|臣狭山命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Sayama no Mikoto|qq=|1=Omisayama no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99517230}}. His children include {{ill|Ōkogashi no Mikoto|ja|大小橋命|1=Ōbashi no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkogashi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbashi no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Maneko no Mikoto|ja|真根子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maneko no Mikoto|qq=|1=Iki no Makoneko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22125699}}, and {{ill|Nihon Daijin no Mikoto|ja|日本大臣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Daijin no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Some accounts mention his marriage to a woman from Baekje or Silla, with their descendants linked to several prominent clans.<ref>*[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * *Chronicles of Japan*, Vol. 1, Iwanami Bunko, 1994. * Ujitani Takeshi (trans.), *Chronicles of Japan*, Kodansha Academic, 1988. * Saeki Arikiyo, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans*, Yuzankaku, 2015. * Kishi Toshio (ed.), *Ancient Japan: Government and Ritual*, Chūō Kōron Bunko, 1996.
== See also == * [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]]

Genealogical texts such as *[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]* identify him as the son of Omi Sayama no Mikoto and father of {{ill|Ōkogashi no Mikoto|ja|大小橋命|1=Ōbashi no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkogashi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbashi no Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Maneko no Mikoto|ja|真根子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maneko no Mikoto|qq=|1=Iki no Makoneko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22125699}}.

According to the *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]]* (vol. 8), upon the sudden death of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] kept his death secret with the help of senior officials, including {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikatsu|ja|中臣烏賊津|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Ikatsu|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōmiwa no Ōtomonushi|ja|大三輪大友主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiwa no Ōtomonushi|qq=|1=Otomoneshi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55536810}}, and others. Ikatsu no Mikoto was tasked with protecting the court during this period.<ref>*[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]], Emperor Chūai, year 9, February 5 entry.</ref>
Later, in vol. 9 of the *Chronicles*, Empress Jingū chose Ikatsu no Mikoto to perform sacred divinations (審神者) during her preparation for an important ritual.<ref>*[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]], Empress Jingū, pre-regency record (Emperor Chūai, year 9, March 1 entry).</ref> His role as a mediator between gods and humans is emphasized, reflecting the meaning of the term "Nakatomi."
Subsequent records in the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]] mention a courtier named Nakatomi no Ikatsu Omimi, who is believed by some scholars to either be a different person with the same name or the same individual as Ikatsu no Mikoto.<ref>*[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]</ref>
== Tsushima Legends ==
Several legends about Ikatsu no Mikoto are preserved on [[:en:Tsushima Island|Tsushima Island]]:<ref>Tsuzuki Tōzō, *Shinto in Tsushima* (San-Ichi Shobō, 1972).</ref> * {{ill|Raijin Shrine|ja|雷神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin Shrine|qq=}} in Izuhara Town commemorates Ikatsu no Mikoto as the figure who managed foreign tribute after returning from Silla. * {{ill|Raimyo Shrine|ja|雷命神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raimyo Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Izuhara is linked to his descendants. * {{ill|Tai Norito Shrine|ja|太祝詞神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tai Norito Shrine|qq=}} in Mitsushima Town is believed to house his grave. * {{ill|Hekireki Shrine|ja|霹靂神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hekireki Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} marks the place of his arrival from Silla. * {{ill|Norito Shrine|ja|能理刀神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Norito Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is where he is said to have performed divinations.
== Genealogy ==
Ikatsu no Mikoto is identified as the son of {{ill|Omi Sayama no Mikoto|ja|臣狭山命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Sayama no Mikoto|qq=|1=Omisayama no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99517230}}. His children include {{ill|Ōkogashi no Mikoto|ja|大小橋命|1=Ōbashi no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkogashi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbashi no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Maneko no Mikoto|ja|真根子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maneko no Mikoto|qq=|1=Iki no Makoneko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22125699}}, and {{ill|Nihon Daijin no Mikoto|ja|日本大臣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Daijin no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Some accounts mention his marriage to a woman from Baekje or Silla, with their descendants linked to several prominent clans.<ref>*[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]</ref>

'''Ikatsu no Mikoto''' (中臣 烏賊津) is a legendary noble and regarded as the founder of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣連|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣氏}} from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Although not mentioned in the *[[:en:Kojiki|Records of Ancient Matters]]*, he is referred to in the *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]]* under various names such as '''Nakatomi no Ikatsu Omimi''' and '''Rai Daijin no Mikoto'''. Genealogical texts such as *[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]* identify him as the son of Omi Sayama no Mikoto and father of {{ill|Ōkogashi no Mikoto|ja|大小橋命|1=Ōbashi no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkogashi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbashi no Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Maneko no Mikoto|ja|真根子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maneko no Mikoto|qq=|1=Iki no Makoneko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22125699}}.

According to the *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]]* (vol. 8), upon the sudden death of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] kept his death secret with the help of senior officials, including {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikatsu|ja|中臣烏賊津|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Ikatsu|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōmiwa no Ōtomonushi|ja|大三輪大友主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiwa no Ōtomonushi|qq=|1=Otomoneshi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55536810}}, and others. Ikatsu no Mikoto was tasked with protecting the court during this period.<ref>*[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]], Emperor Chūai, year 9, February 5 entry.</ref>
Later, in vol. 9 of the *Chronicles*, Empress Jingū chose Ikatsu no Mikoto to perform sacred divinations (審神者) during her preparation for an important ritual.<ref>*[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]], Empress Jingū, pre-regency record (Emperor Chūai, year 9, March 1 entry).</ref> His role as a mediator between gods and humans is emphasized, reflecting the meaning of the term "Nakatomi."
Subsequent records in the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]] mention a courtier named Nakatomi no Ikatsu Omimi, who is believed by some scholars to either be a different person with the same name or the same individual as Ikatsu no Mikoto.<ref>*[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]</ref>

Several legends about Ikatsu no Mikoto are preserved on [[:en:Tsushima Island|Tsushima Island]]:<ref>Tsuzuki Tōzō, *Shinto in Tsushima* (San-Ichi Shobō, 1972).</ref> * {{ill|Raijin Shrine|ja|雷神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin Shrine|qq=}} in Izuhara Town commemorates Ikatsu no Mikoto as the figure who managed foreign tribute after returning from Silla. * {{ill|Raimyo Shrine|ja|雷命神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raimyo Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Izuhara is linked to his descendants. * {{ill|Tai Norito Shrine|ja|太祝詞神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tai Norito Shrine|qq=}} in Mitsushima Town is believed to house his grave. * {{ill|Hekireki Shrine|ja|霹靂神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hekireki Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} marks the place of his arrival from Silla. * {{ill|Norito Shrine|ja|能理刀神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Norito Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is where he is said to have performed divinations.

Ikatsu no Mikoto is identified as the son of {{ill|Omi Sayama no Mikoto|ja|臣狭山命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Sayama no Mikoto|qq=|1=Omisayama no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99517230}}. His children include {{ill|Ōkogashi no Mikoto|ja|大小橋命|1=Ōbashi no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkogashi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbashi no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Maneko no Mikoto|ja|真根子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maneko no Mikoto|qq=|1=Iki no Makoneko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22125699}}, and {{ill|Nihon Daijin no Mikoto|ja|日本大臣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Daijin no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. '''Ikatsu no Mikoto''' (中臣 烏賊津) is a legendary noble and regarded as the founder of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣氏}} from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Although not mentioned in the *[[:en:Kojiki|Records of Ancient Matters]]*, he is referred to in the *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]]* under various names such as '''Nakatomi no Ikatsu Omimi''' and '''Rai Daijin no Mikoto'''. Genealogical texts such as *[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]* identify him as the son of Omi Sayama no Mikoto and father of {{ill|Ōkogashi no Mikoto|ja|大小橋命|1=Ōbashi no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkogashi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbashi no Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Maneko no Mikoto|ja|真根子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maneko no Mikoto|1=Iki no Makoneko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22125699}}.
== Records ==
According to the *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]]* (vol. 8), upon the sudden death of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] kept his death secret with the help of senior officials, including {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikatsu|ja|中臣烏賊津|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Ikatsu|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōmiwa no Ōtomonushi|ja|大三輪大友主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiwa no Ōtomonushi|1=Otomoneshi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55536810}}, and others. Ikatsu no Mikoto was tasked with protecting the court during this period.<ref>*[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]], Emperor Chūai, year 9, February 5 entry.</ref>
Later, in vol. 9 of the *Chronicles*, Empress Jingū chose Ikatsu no Mikoto to perform sacred divinations (審神者) during her preparation for an important ritual.<ref>*[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]], Empress Jingū, pre-regency record (Emperor Chūai, year 9, March 1 entry).</ref> His role as a mediator between gods and humans is emphasized, reflecting the meaning of the term "Nakatomi."
Subsequent records in the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]] mention a courtier named Nakatomi no Ikatsu Omimi, who is believed by some scholars to either be a different person with the same name or the same individual as Ikatsu no Mikoto.<ref>*[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]</ref>
== Tsushima Legends ==
Several legends about Ikatsu no Mikoto are preserved on [[:en:Tsushima Island|Tsushima Island]]:<ref>Tsuzuki Tōzō, *Shinto in Tsushima* (San-Ichi Shobō, 1972).</ref> * {{ill|Raijin Shrine|ja|雷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin Shrine|qq=}} in Izuhara Town commemorates Ikatsu no Mikoto as the figure who managed foreign tribute after returning from Silla. * {{ill|Raimyo Shrine|ja|雷命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raimyo Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Izuhara is linked to his descendants. * {{ill|Tai Norito Shrine|ja|太祝詞神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tai Norito Shrine|qq=}} in Mitsushima Town is believed to house his grave. * {{ill|Hekireki Shrine|ja|霹靂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hekireki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} marks the place of his arrival from Silla. * {{ill|Norito Shrine|ja|能理刀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Norito Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is where he is said to have performed divinations.
== Genealogy ==
Ikatsu no Mikoto is identified as the son of {{ill|Omi Sayama no Mikoto|ja|臣狭山命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Sayama no Mikoto|1=Omisayama no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99517230}}. His children include {{ill|Ōkogashi no Mikoto|ja|大小橋命|1=Ōbashi no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkogashi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbashi no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Maneko no Mikoto|ja|真根子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maneko no Mikoto|1=Iki no Makoneko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22125699}}, and {{ill|Nihon Daijin no Mikoto|ja|日本大臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Daijin no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Some accounts mention his marriage to a woman from Baekje or Silla, with their descendants linked to several prominent clans.<ref>*[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * *Chronicles of Japan*, Vol. 1, Iwanami Bunko, 1994. * Ujitani Takeshi (trans.), *Chronicles of Japan*, Kodansha Academic, 1988. * Saeki Arikiyo, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans*, Yuzankaku, 2015. * Kishi Toshio (ed.), *Ancient Japan: Government and Ritual*, Chūō Kōron Bunko, 1996.
== See also == * [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]]

According to the *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]]* (vol. 8), upon the sudden death of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] kept his death secret with the help of senior officials, including {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikatsu|ja|中臣烏賊津|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Ikatsu|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōmiwa no Ōtomonushi|ja|大三輪大友主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiwa no Ōtomonushi|1=Otomoneshi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55536810}}, and others. Ikatsu no Mikoto was tasked with protecting the court during this period.<ref>*[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]], Emperor Chūai, year 9, February 5 entry.</ref>
Later, in vol. 9 of the *Chronicles*, Empress Jingū chose Ikatsu no Mikoto to perform sacred divinations (審神者) during her preparation for an important ritual.<ref>*[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]], Empress Jingū, pre-regency record (Emperor Chūai, year 9, March 1 entry).</ref> His role as a mediator between gods and humans is emphasized, reflecting the meaning of the term "Nakatomi."
Subsequent records in the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]] mention a courtier named Nakatomi no Ikatsu Omimi, who is believed by some scholars to either be a different person with the same name or the same individual as Ikatsu no Mikoto.<ref>*[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]</ref>
== Tsushima Legends ==
Several legends about Ikatsu no Mikoto are preserved on [[:en:Tsushima Island|Tsushima Island]]:<ref>Tsuzuki Tōzō, *Shinto in Tsushima* (San-Ichi Shobō, 1972).</ref> * {{ill|Raijin Shrine|ja|雷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin Shrine|qq=}} in Izuhara Town commemorates Ikatsu no Mikoto as the figure who managed foreign tribute after returning from Silla. * {{ill|Raimyo Shrine|ja|雷命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raimyo Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Izuhara is linked to his descendants. * {{ill|Tai Norito Shrine|ja|太祝詞神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tai Norito Shrine|qq=}} in Mitsushima Town is believed to house his grave. * {{ill|Hekireki Shrine|ja|霹靂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hekireki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} marks the place of his arrival from Silla. * {{ill|Norito Shrine|ja|能理刀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Norito Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is where he is said to have performed divinations.
== Genealogy ==
Ikatsu no Mikoto is identified as the son of {{ill|Omi Sayama no Mikoto|ja|臣狭山命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Sayama no Mikoto|1=Omisayama no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99517230}}. His children include {{ill|Ōkogashi no Mikoto|ja|大小橋命|1=Ōbashi no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkogashi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbashi no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Maneko no Mikoto|ja|真根子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maneko no Mikoto|1=Iki no Makoneko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22125699}}, and {{ill|Nihon Daijin no Mikoto|ja|日本大臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Daijin no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Some accounts mention his marriage to a woman from Baekje or Silla, with their descendants linked to several prominent clans.<ref>*[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]</ref>

Genealogical texts such as *[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]* identify him as the son of Omi Sayama no Mikoto and father of {{ill|Ōkogashi no Mikoto|ja|大小橋命|1=Ōbashi no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkogashi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbashi no Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Maneko no Mikoto|ja|真根子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maneko no Mikoto|1=Iki no Makoneko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22125699}}.

Later, in vol. 9 of the *Chronicles*, Empress Jingū chose Ikatsu no Mikoto to perform sacred divinations (審神者) during her preparation for an important ritual.<ref>*[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]], Empress Jingū, pre-regency record (Emperor Chūai, year 9, March 1 entry).</ref> His role as a mediator between gods and humans is emphasized, reflecting the meaning of the term "Nakatomi."

| clan = [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]] (Kawachi Province), {{ill|Iki clan|ja|壱岐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki clan|1=Iki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429628}}, Wu nobility in Yamashiro Province, Shinetsu clan in Settsu Province, {{ill|Ikuta clan|ja|生田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikuta clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Settsu Province), {{ill|Nakatomi Kurihara clan|ja|中臣栗原氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi Kurihara clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tsushima clan|ja|津島直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Settsu Province), Mimami clan (Kawachi Province)<ref name="#1">*[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]</ref> | parents = Father: {{ill|Omi Sayama no Mikoto|ja|臣狭山命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Sayama no Mikoto|1=Omisayama no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99517230}}<ref name="#2">*[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]</ref><ref name="#3">Matsuo Shrine Genealogy (in *{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1554852}}*, vol. 181)</ref> | siblings = | spouse = Woman of Baekje (or Silla) | children = {{ill|Ōkogashi no Mikoto|ja|大小橋命|1=Ōbashi no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkogashi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbashi no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Maneko no Mikoto|ja|真根子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maneko no Mikoto|1=Iki no Makoneko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22125699}}, {{ill|Dainishi no Mikoto|ja|弟子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainishi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="#1"/>, {{ill|Nihon Daijin no Mikoto|ja|日本大臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Daijin no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="#2"/><ref name="#3"/> }}
'''Ikatsu no Mikoto''' (中臣 烏賊津) is a legendary noble and regarded as the founder of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣氏}} from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Although not mentioned in the *[[:en:Kojiki|Records of Ancient Matters]]*, he is referred to in the *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]]* under various names such as '''Nakatomi no Ikatsu Omimi''' and '''Rai Daijin no Mikoto'''. Genealogical texts such as *[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]* identify him as the son of Omi Sayama no Mikoto and father of {{ill|Ōkogashi no Mikoto|ja|大小橋命|1=Ōbashi no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkogashi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbashi no Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Maneko no Mikoto|ja|真根子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maneko no Mikoto|1=Iki no Makoneko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22125699}}.
== Records ==
According to the *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]]* (vol. 8), upon the sudden death of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] kept his death secret with the help of senior officials, including {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikatsu|ja|中臣烏賊津|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Ikatsu|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōmiwa no Ōtomonushi|ja|大三輪大友主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiwa no Ōtomonushi|1=Otomoneshi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55536810}}, and others. Ikatsu no Mikoto was tasked with protecting the court during this period.<ref>*[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]], Emperor Chūai, year 9, February 5 entry.</ref>
Later, in vol. 9 of the *Chronicles*, Empress Jingū chose Ikatsu no Mikoto to perform sacred divinations (審神者) during her preparation for an important ritual.<ref>*[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]], Empress Jingū, pre-regency record (Emperor Chūai, year 9, March 1 entry).</ref> His role as a mediator between gods and humans is emphasized, reflecting the meaning of the term "Nakatomi."
Subsequent records in the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]] mention a courtier named Nakatomi no Ikatsu Omimi, who is believed by some scholars to either be a different person with the same name or the same individual as Ikatsu no Mikoto.<ref>*[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]</ref>
== Tsushima Legends ==
Several legends about Ikatsu no Mikoto are preserved on [[:en:Tsushima Island|Tsushima Island]]:<ref>Tsuzuki Tōzō, *Shinto in Tsushima* (San-Ichi Shobō, 1972).</ref> * {{ill|Raijin Shrine|ja|雷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin Shrine|qq=}} in Izuhara Town commemorates Ikatsu no Mikoto as the figure who managed foreign tribute after returning from Silla. * {{ill|Raimyo Shrine|ja|雷命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raimyo Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Izuhara is linked to his descendants. * {{ill|Tai Norito Shrine|ja|太祝詞神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tai Norito Shrine|qq=}} in Mitsushima Town is believed to house his grave. * {{ill|Hekireki Shrine|ja|霹靂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hekireki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} marks the place of his arrival from Silla. * {{ill|Norito Shrine|ja|能理刀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Norito Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is where he is said to have performed divinations.
== Genealogy ==
Ikatsu no Mikoto is identified as the son of {{ill|Omi Sayama no Mikoto|ja|臣狭山命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Sayama no Mikoto|1=Omisayama no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99517230}}. His children include {{ill|Ōkogashi no Mikoto|ja|大小橋命|1=Ōbashi no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkogashi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbashi no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Maneko no Mikoto|ja|真根子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maneko no Mikoto|1=Iki no Makoneko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22125699}}, and {{ill|Nihon Daijin no Mikoto|ja|日本大臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Daijin no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Some accounts mention his marriage to a woman from Baekje or Silla, with their descendants linked to several prominent clans.<ref>*[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * *Chronicles of Japan*, Vol. 1, Iwanami Bunko, 1994. * Ujitani Takeshi (trans.), *Chronicles of Japan*, Kodansha Academic, 1988. * Saeki Arikiyo, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans*, Yuzankaku, 2015. * Kishi Toshio (ed.), *Ancient Japan: Government and Ritual*, Chūō Kōron Bunko, 1996.
== See also == * [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]]

'''Ikatsu no Mikoto''' (中臣 烏賊津) is a legendary noble and regarded as the founder of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣氏}} from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Although not mentioned in the *[[:en:Kojiki|Records of Ancient Matters]]*, he is referred to in the *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]]* under various names such as '''Nakatomi no Ikatsu Omimi''' and '''Rai Daijin no Mikoto'''. Genealogical texts such as *[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]* identify him as the son of Omi Sayama no Mikoto and father of {{ill|Ōkogashi no Mikoto|ja|大小橋命|1=Ōbashi no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkogashi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbashi no Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Maneko no Mikoto|ja|真根子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maneko no Mikoto|1=Iki no Makoneko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22125699}}.
== Records ==
According to the *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]]* (vol. 8), upon the sudden death of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] kept his death secret with the help of senior officials, including {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikatsu|ja|中臣烏賊津|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Ikatsu|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōmiwa no Ōtomonushi|ja|大三輪大友主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiwa no Ōtomonushi|1=Otomoneshi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55536810}}, and others. Ikatsu no Mikoto was tasked with protecting the court during this period.<ref>*[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]], Emperor Chūai, year 9, February 5 entry.</ref>
Later, in vol. 9 of the *Chronicles*, Empress Jingū chose Ikatsu no Mikoto to perform sacred divinations (審神者) during her preparation for an important ritual.<ref>*[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]], Empress Jingū, pre-regency record (Emperor Chūai, year 9, March 1 entry).</ref> His role as a mediator between gods and humans is emphasized, reflecting the meaning of the term "Nakatomi."
Subsequent records in the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]] mention a courtier named Nakatomi no Ikatsu Omimi, who is believed by some scholars to either be a different person with the same name or the same individual as Ikatsu no Mikoto.<ref>*[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]</ref>
== Tsushima Legends ==
Several legends about Ikatsu no Mikoto are preserved on [[:en:Tsushima Island|Tsushima Island]]:<ref>Tsuzuki Tōzō, *Shinto in Tsushima* (San-Ichi Shobō, 1972).</ref> * {{ill|Raijin Shrine|ja|雷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin Shrine|qq=}} in Izuhara Town commemorates Ikatsu no Mikoto as the figure who managed foreign tribute after returning from Silla. * {{ill|Raimyo Shrine|ja|雷命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raimyo Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Izuhara is linked to his descendants. * {{ill|Tai Norito Shrine|ja|太祝詞神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tai Norito Shrine|qq=}} in Mitsushima Town is believed to house his grave. * {{ill|Hekireki Shrine|ja|霹靂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hekireki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} marks the place of his arrival from Silla. * {{ill|Norito Shrine|ja|能理刀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Norito Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is where he is said to have performed divinations.
== Genealogy ==
Ikatsu no Mikoto is identified as the son of {{ill|Omi Sayama no Mikoto|ja|臣狭山命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Sayama no Mikoto|1=Omisayama no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99517230}}. His children include {{ill|Ōkogashi no Mikoto|ja|大小橋命|1=Ōbashi no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkogashi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbashi no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Maneko no Mikoto|ja|真根子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maneko no Mikoto|1=Iki no Makoneko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22125699}}, and {{ill|Nihon Daijin no Mikoto|ja|日本大臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Daijin no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Some accounts mention his marriage to a woman from Baekje or Silla, with their descendants linked to several prominent clans.<ref>*[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * *Chronicles of Japan*, Vol. 1, Iwanami Bunko, 1994. * Ujitani Takeshi (trans.), *Chronicles of Japan*, Kodansha Academic, 1988. * Saeki Arikiyo, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans*, Yuzankaku, 2015. * Kishi Toshio (ed.), *Ancient Japan: Government and Ritual*, Chūō Kōron Bunko, 1996.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:中臣烏賊津]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:中臣烏賊津]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:中臣烏賊津]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|spouse={{ill|Q108616853|WD=Q108616853}}|children={{ill|Q22125699|WD=Q22125699}}; {{ill|Ōbase no Mikoto|WD=Q99517235}}|father={{ill|Omisayama no Mikoto|WD=Q99517230}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55536814]] Wikidata name: Nakatomi no Ikatsu [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Ikatsu no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Nakatomi no Ikatsu, Ikatsu Omimi, Rai Daijin no Mikoto | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | Rank = | lord = [[Emperor Chūai]], [[Empress Jingū]], [[Emperor Ingyō]]? | clan = [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]] (Kawachi Province), {{ill|Iki clan|ja|壱岐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki clan|1=Iki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429628}}, Wu nobility in Yamashiro Province, Shinetsu clan in Settsu Province, {{ill|Ikuta clan|ja|生田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikuta clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Settsu Province), {{ill|Nakatomi Kurihara clan|ja|中臣栗原氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi Kurihara clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tsushima clan|ja|津島直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Settsu Province), Mimami clan (Kawachi Province)<ref name="#1">*[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]</ref> | parents = Father: {{ill|Omi Sayama no Mikoto|ja|臣狭山命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Sayama no Mikoto|1=Omisayama no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99517230}}<ref name="#2">*[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]</ref><ref name="#3">Matsuo Shrine Genealogy (in *{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1554852}}*, vol. 181)</ref> | siblings = | spouse = Woman of Baekje (or Silla) | children = {{ill|Ōkogashi no Mikoto|ja|大小橋命|1=Ōbashi no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkogashi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbashi no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Maneko no Mikoto|ja|真根子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maneko no Mikoto|1=Iki no Makoneko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22125699}}, {{ill|Dainishi no Mikoto|ja|弟子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainishi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="#1"/>, {{ill|Nihon Daijin no Mikoto|ja|日本大臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Daijin no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="#2"/><ref name="#3"/> }}
'''Ikatsu no Mikoto''' (中臣 烏賊津) is a legendary noble and regarded as the founder of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣氏}} from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Although not mentioned in the *[[:en:Kojiki|Records of Ancient Matters]]*, he is referred to in the *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]]* under various names such as '''Nakatomi no Ikatsu Omimi''' and '''Rai Daijin no Mikoto'''. Genealogical texts such as *[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]* identify him as the son of Omi Sayama no Mikoto and father of {{ill|Ōkogashi no Mikoto|ja|大小橋命|1=Ōbashi no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkogashi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbashi no Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Maneko no Mikoto|ja|真根子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maneko no Mikoto|1=Iki no Makoneko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22125699}}.
== Records ==
According to the *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]]* (vol. 8), upon the sudden death of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] kept his death secret with the help of senior officials, including {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikatsu|ja|中臣烏賊津|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Ikatsu|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōmiwa no Ōtomonushi|ja|大三輪大友主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiwa no Ōtomonushi|1=Otomoneshi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55536810}}, and others. Ikatsu no Mikoto was tasked with protecting the court during this period.<ref>*[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]], Emperor Chūai, year 9, February 5 entry.</ref>
Later, in vol. 9 of the *Chronicles*, Empress Jingū chose Ikatsu no Mikoto to perform sacred divinations (審神者) during her preparation for an important ritual.<ref>*[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]], Empress Jingū, pre-regency record (Emperor Chūai, year 9, March 1 entry).</ref> His role as a mediator between gods and humans is emphasized, reflecting the meaning of the term "Nakatomi."
Subsequent records in the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]] mention a courtier named Nakatomi no Ikatsu Omimi, who is believed by some scholars to either be a different person with the same name or the same individual as Ikatsu no Mikoto.<ref>*[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]</ref>
== Tsushima Legends ==
Several legends about Ikatsu no Mikoto are preserved on [[:en:Tsushima Island|Tsushima Island]]:<ref>Tsuzuki Tōzō, *Shinto in Tsushima* (San-Ichi Shobō, 1972).</ref> * {{ill|Raijin Shrine|ja|雷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin Shrine|qq=}} in Izuhara Town commemorates Ikatsu no Mikoto as the figure who managed foreign tribute after returning from Silla. * {{ill|Raimyo Shrine|ja|雷命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raimyo Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Izuhara is linked to his descendants. * {{ill|Tai Norito Shrine|ja|太祝詞神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tai Norito Shrine|qq=}} in Mitsushima Town is believed to house his grave. * {{ill|Hekireki Shrine|ja|霹靂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hekireki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} marks the place of his arrival from Silla. * {{ill|Norito Shrine|ja|能理刀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Norito Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is where he is said to have performed divinations.
== Genealogy ==
Ikatsu no Mikoto is identified as the son of {{ill|Omi Sayama no Mikoto|ja|臣狭山命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Sayama no Mikoto|1=Omisayama no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99517230}}. His children include {{ill|Ōkogashi no Mikoto|ja|大小橋命|1=Ōbashi no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkogashi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbashi no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Maneko no Mikoto|ja|真根子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maneko no Mikoto|1=Iki no Makoneko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22125699}}, and {{ill|Nihon Daijin no Mikoto|ja|日本大臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Daijin no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Some accounts mention his marriage to a woman from Baekje or Silla, with their descendants linked to several prominent clans.<ref>*[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * *Chronicles of Japan*, Vol. 1, Iwanami Bunko, 1994. * Ujitani Takeshi (trans.), *Chronicles of Japan*, Kodansha Academic, 1988. * Saeki Arikiyo, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans*, Yuzankaku, 2015. * Kishi Toshio (ed.), *Ancient Japan: Government and Ritual*, Chūō Kōron Bunko, 1996.
== See also == * [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]]

'''Ikatsu no Mikoto''' (中臣 烏賊津) is a legendary noble and regarded as the founder of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣氏}} from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Although not mentioned in the *[[:en:Kojiki|Records of Ancient Matters]]*, he is referred to in the *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]]* under various names such as '''Nakatomi no Ikatsu Omimi''' and '''Rai Daijin no Mikoto'''. Genealogical texts such as *[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]* identify him as the son of Omi Sayama no Mikoto and father of {{ill|Ōkogashi no Mikoto|ja|大小橋命|1=Ōbashi no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkogashi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbashi no Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Maneko no Mikoto|ja|真根子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maneko no Mikoto|1=Iki no Makoneko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22125699}}.
== Records ==
According to the *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]]* (vol. 8), upon the sudden death of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] kept his death secret with the help of senior officials, including {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikatsu|ja|中臣烏賊津|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Ikatsu|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōmiwa no Ōtomonushi|ja|大三輪大友主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiwa no Ōtomonushi|1=Otomoneshi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55536810}}, and others. Ikatsu no Mikoto was tasked with protecting the court during this period.<ref>*[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]], Emperor Chūai, year 9, February 5 entry.</ref>
Later, in vol. 9 of the *Chronicles*, Empress Jingū chose Ikatsu no Mikoto to perform sacred divinations (審神者) during her preparation for an important ritual.<ref>*[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]], Empress Jingū, pre-regency record (Emperor Chūai, year 9, March 1 entry).</ref> His role as a mediator between gods and humans is emphasized, reflecting the meaning of the term "Nakatomi."
Subsequent records in the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]] mention a courtier named Nakatomi no Ikatsu Omimi, who is believed by some scholars to either be a different person with the same name or the same individual as Ikatsu no Mikoto.<ref>*[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]</ref>
== Tsushima Legends ==
Several legends about Ikatsu no Mikoto are preserved on [[:en:Tsushima Island|Tsushima Island]]:<ref>Tsuzuki Tōzō, *Shinto in Tsushima* (San-Ichi Shobō, 1972).</ref> * {{ill|Raijin Shrine|ja|雷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin Shrine|qq=}} in Izuhara Town commemorates Ikatsu no Mikoto as the figure who managed foreign tribute after returning from Silla. * {{ill|Raimyo Shrine|ja|雷命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raimyo Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Izuhara is linked to his descendants. * {{ill|Tai Norito Shrine|ja|太祝詞神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tai Norito Shrine|qq=}} in Mitsushima Town is believed to house his grave. * {{ill|Hekireki Shrine|ja|霹靂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hekireki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} marks the place of his arrival from Silla. * {{ill|Norito Shrine|ja|能理刀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Norito Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is where he is said to have performed divinations.
== Genealogy ==
Ikatsu no Mikoto is identified as the son of {{ill|Omi Sayama no Mikoto|ja|臣狭山命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Sayama no Mikoto|1=Omisayama no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99517230}}. His children include {{ill|Ōkogashi no Mikoto|ja|大小橋命|1=Ōbashi no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkogashi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbashi no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Maneko no Mikoto|ja|真根子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maneko no Mikoto|1=Iki no Makoneko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22125699}}, and {{ill|Nihon Daijin no Mikoto|ja|日本大臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Daijin no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Some accounts mention his marriage to a woman from Baekje or Silla, with their descendants linked to several prominent clans.<ref>*[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * *Chronicles of Japan*, Vol. 1, Iwanami Bunko, 1994. * Ujitani Takeshi (trans.), *Chronicles of Japan*, Kodansha Academic, 1988. * Saeki Arikiyo, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans*, Yuzankaku, 2015. * Kishi Toshio (ed.), *Ancient Japan: Government and Ritual*, Chūō Kōron Bunko, 1996.
